title: Monitoring & drift (data / concept / label) + DSA 1-D DP
focus: PSI / KS / JS for drift; closed-loop retraining; delayed labels.

items.label: Data drift, concept drift, label drift
interviewQuestions: Compare data drift vs concept drift vs label drift — give an example of each.
Walk through three statistical tests you'd use to detect drift (PSI, KS, JS).

items.label: Monitoring stack: predictions, latency, errors
interviewQuestions: What metrics do you monitor for a deployed model beyond accuracy?
How would you slice your model monitoring dashboard so a regression in one cohort doesn't get masked?

items.label: Closed-loop monitoring & retraining triggers
interviewQuestions: How would you decide when to retrain — schedule vs trigger-based?
How do you monitor when ground-truth labels arrive with delay?

tracks.label: Production ML · Incident response and model rollback

items.label: Incident runbooks for drift, bad labels, broken features, and serving regressions
interviewQuestions: A model starts hurting one cohort but aggregate metrics look flat. What is your incident response?
How do you decide between rollback, threshold change, feature disablement, and retraining?
What postmortem action prevents the same model incident from recurring?

items.label: Delayed-label monitoring: proxies, holdouts, and retrospective truth joins
interviewQuestions: How do you monitor fraud, churn, or recommendation quality when labels arrive weeks later?
Which proxy metrics are safe to use temporarily, and how can they mislead you?
How do you join delayed labels back to the exact prediction context?

references.label: Chip Huyen — Machine Learning Systems Design
